[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 搜神后记 - Title meaning 'Supplementary Records of the Supernatural,' indicating a collection of tales about gods, spirits, and the supernatural.
  • 本 - Means 'originally' or 'by birth,' used here to indicate origin or ancestry.
  • 学道 - Refers to the practice of Daoist cultivation or the pursuit of the Dao (Way).
  • 灵虚山 - A specific mountain name, likely a Daoist sacred site, where the protagonist studied.
  • 化鹤 - A metaphor for transformation into a crane, symbolizing immortality or spiritual transcendence.
  • 徘徊空中而言 - Describes the crane circling in the air and speaking, a common motif in Daoist and Buddhist tales of immortals.
  • 城郭如故人民非 - A phrase expressing the idea that while the physical structures of a city remain unchanged, the people have changed over time.
  • 高上冲天 - Means 'soaring high into the sky,' a metaphor for ascending to heaven or achieving immortality.
  • 冢垒垒 - Refers to grave mounds or burial sites, often used to symbolize the passage of time and the mortality of humans.
  • 有鸟有鸟丁令威 - A poetic refrain, repeating the name 'Ding Lingwei' to emphasize the immortality and return of the protagonist.
  • 嵩高山 - The Songshan Mountains, a famous range in central China, often associated with Daoist and Buddhist traditions.
  • 大穴 - Refers to a large cave or pit, possibly a natural formation or a Daoist hermitage.
  • 莫测其深 - Means 'impossible to measure its depth,' emphasizing the mystery and vastness of the cave.
  • 百姓岁时游观 - Means 'common people visit and observe during festivals,' indicating the cave's cultural significance.
  • 投食于穴中 - Describes the act of throwing food into the cave, a gesture of goodwill or curiosity.
  • 玉浆 - Refers to a mythical drink of jade essence, often associated with immortality in Daoist texts.
  • 龙穴石髓 - Refers to a mythical substance found in dragon caves, often associated with longevity and immortality.
  • 天井 - Refers to a natural or artificial well, often used in Daoist texts to symbolize a portal to the underworld or a hidden realm.
  • 蛟龙 - Refers to water dragons, mythical creatures often associated with the depths of rivers and lakes.
  • 投身入井 - Means 'to throw oneself into the well,' a metaphor for a leap of faith or a desperate act.
  • 蜀中 - Refers to the region of Sichuan, known for its rugged terrain and rich cultural history.
  • 归洛下 - Means 'returning to Luoyang,' indicating the protagonist's journey back to a familiar place.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 丁令威 - A legendary figure in Chinese folklore, known for his transformation into a crane and his return to his hometown after a long absence.
  • 辽东 - A historical region in northeastern China, known for its rich cultural and natural heritage.
  • 灵虚山 - A specific mountain, likely a Daoist sacred site, where the protagonist studied.
  • 嵩高山 - The Songshan Mountains, a famous range in central China, often associated with Daoist and Buddhist traditions.
  • 张华 - A historical figure, a scholar and official during the Western Jin dynasty, known for his knowledge and wisdom.
  • 晋 - Refers to the Western Jin dynasty, a period of Chinese history marked by political instability and cultural flourishing.
  • 陶潜 - The author of the text, a renowned poet and writer from the Eastern Jin dynasty, known for his works on Daoist themes and his reclusive lifestyle.

Proper Nouns

  • 搜神后记 - The title of the text.
  • 晋 - The Western Jin dynasty.
  • 陶潜 - The author of the text.
  • 丁令威 - The legendary figure in the first story.
  • 辽东 - The historical region where the first story takes place.
  • 灵虚山 - The mountain where the protagonist studied.
  • 嵩高山 - The Songshan Mountains.
  • 张华 - A historical figure and scholar.
  • 蜀中 - The region of Sichuan.
  • 洛下 - Luoyang, the capital city.
  • 仙馆大夫 - A term for high-ranking immortals in the celestial bureaucracy.
  • 玉浆 - A mythical drink of jade essence.
  • 龙穴石髓 - A mythical substance found in dragon caves.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 深山重岭 - Describes a region with deep mountains and multiple ridges, emphasizing the rugged and remote setting.
  • 石桥 - A stone bridge, which is a common feature in ancient Chinese literature, often symbolizing a threshold or boundary.
  • 绝崖 - A sheer cliff, indicating a dramatic and dangerous landscape.
  • 赤城 - A mountain in Zhejiang, known for its red cliffs and scenic beauty. The name '赤城' (Red City) is a poetic reference to the mountain's appearance.
  • 瀑布 - A waterfall, which is a common natural feature in Chinese literature, often associated with beauty and tranquility.
  • 山穴 - A mountain cave, which can be a place of mystery and transformation.
  • 豁然 - Describes a sudden and clear opening, often used to convey a sense of discovery or revelation.
  • 草木皆香 - All plants and trees emit a fragrance, suggesting a paradisiacal environment.
  • 小屋 - A small house, which in this context is likely a simple dwelling.
  • 二女子 - Two women, who are described as having names (莹珠 and 洁玉), indicating they are significant characters.
  • 室家 - A household or family, suggesting a marital relationship.
  • 得婿者 - Someone who has found a husband, implying a social event or celebration.
  • 绝岩 - A sheer cliff, emphasizing the dangerous and inaccessible nature of the landscape.
  • 琅琅然 - Describes a clear and resonant sound, often used to describe the sound of footsteps or other actions.
  • 慎勿开也 - Be careful not to open it, indicating a warning or prohibition.
  • 囊 - A bag or pouch, which in this context is a magical or mystical object.
  • 一重去，一重复 - One layer after another, describing the structure of the bag.
  • 小青鸟 - A small green bird, which is a common symbol in Chinese literature, often representing freedom or a messenger.
  • 田中耕 - Working in the fields, indicating agricultural labor.
  • 壳如蝉蜕 - A shell like a cicada's molt, suggesting a transformation or shedding of an old form.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 会稽剡县 - Yandian County in Kuaiji, a region known for its natural beauty and historical significance.
  • 赤城 - A famous mountain in Zhejiang, known for its red cliffs and scenic beauty.
  • 瀑布 - Waterfalls are often associated with natural beauty and spiritual significance in Chinese literature.
  • 山穴 - Caves are often depicted as places of mystery and transformation, sometimes leading to otherworldly realms.
  • 小青鸟 - In Chinese mythology, small green birds are often associated with messengers or symbols of freedom and transformation.

Proper Nouns

  • 袁相 - A name, likely a character in the story.
  • 根硕 - A name, likely a character in the story.
  • 莹珠 - A name, likely a character in the story.
  • 洁玉 - A name, likely a character in the story.
  • 赤城 - A mountain in Zhejiang, known for its red cliffs and scenic beauty.
  • 瀑布 - A waterfall, which is a common natural feature in Chinese literature.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 隐遁养志 - A lifestyle of withdrawal and self-cultivation, common among scholars and recluses.
  • 世业 - Family legacy, indicating the long-term impact on the family.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 荥阳 - A place name, indicating the person's origin.
  • 荆州 - An ancient Chinese province, indicating the location of the appointment.
  • 别驾 - A high-ranking official position, indicating the importance of the offer.
  • 韶舞 - An ancient dance, often associated with the Yellow Emperor and the Five Emperors, symbolizing harmony and grace.
  • 良田数十顷 - Several hundred acres of good land, indicating a significant amount of fertile land.
  • 子孙至今赖之 - Descendants still rely on it, indicating the lasting benefit.

Proper Nouns

  • 荥阳 - A place name.
  • 荆州 - An ancient Chinese province.
  • 别驾 - A high-ranking official position.
  • 韶舞 - An ancient dance.
  • 良田数十顷 - Several hundred acres of good land.
  • 世业 - Family legacy.
  • 子孙至今赖之 - Descendants still rely on it.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 晋太元中 - Refers to the reign period of Emperor Hui of Jin (376–386 CE).
  • 捕鱼为业 - Means 'engaged in fishing as a profession.'
  • 缘溪行 - Means 'following the stream.'
  • 忘路之远近 - Means 'forgetting how far or near the road was.'
  • 忽逢桃花林 - Means 'suddenly came upon a peach blossom forest.'
  • 夹岸数百步 - Means 'on both banks for several hundred steps.'
  • 中无杂树 - Means 'no mixed trees in between.'
  • 芳华鲜美，落英缤纷 - Describes the beautiful flowers and the falling petals.
  • 渔人甚异之 - Means 'the fisherman found it very strange.'
  • 复前行，欲穷其林 - Means 'went forward again, wanting to explore the entire forest.'
  • 林尽水源 - Means 'the forest ended at the water source.'
  • 便得一山 - Means 'and found a mountain.'
  • 山有小口，彷佛若有光 - Means 'there was a small opening in the mountain, as if there was light inside.'
  • 便舍舟，从口入 - Means 'left his boat and entered through the opening.'
  • 初极狭，才通人 - Means 'it was very narrow at first, barely wide enough for a person to pass through.'
  • 复行数十步，豁然开朗 - Means 'after walking several dozen steps, it suddenly opened up into an open space.'
  • 土地旷空，屋舍俨然 - Describes the open land and neatly arranged houses.
  • 有良田、美池、桑竹之属 - Means 'there were good fields, beautiful ponds, mulberry trees, and bamboo.'
  • 阡陌交通，鸡犬相闻 - Describes the well-connected paths and the sounds of chickens and dogs.
  • 男女衣著，悉如外人 - Means 'the men and women were dressed just like those outside.'
  • 黄发垂髫，并怡然自乐 - Means 'the elderly and children were all happy and content.'
  • 见渔人，大惊，问所从来，具答之 - Means 'they saw the fisherman and were greatly surprised, asking where he came from, and he answered them in detail.'
  • 便要还家，为设酒杀鸡作食 - Means 'they invited him to return home, preparing wine, killing a chicken, and cooking food for him.'
  • 村中人闻有此人，咸来问讯 - Means 'the villagers heard about this person and all came to inquire.'
  • 自云先世避秦难，率妻子邑人至此绝境，不复出焉，遂与外隔 - Means 'they said their ancestors fled from the Qin troubles, leading their wives, children, and fellow townspeople to this isolated place, never leaving again, thus being cut off from the outside world.'
  • 问今是何世，乃不知有汉，无论魏晋 - Means 'they asked what era it was now, and they did not know about the Han dynasty, let alone the Wei and Jin dynasties.'
  • 此人一一具言所闻，皆为叹惋 - Means 'he told them everything he had heard, and they all sighed in amazement.'
  • 余人各复延至其家，皆出酒食 - Means 'the others also invited him to their homes, all offering wine and food.'
  • 停数曰，辞去 - Means 'he stayed for several days before taking his leave.'
  • 此中人语云：“不足为外人道也” - Means 'the people here told him, 'It is not worth telling outsiders about.''
  • 既出，得其船，便扶向路，处处志之 - Means 'after leaving, he found his boat, retraced his steps, and marked the path everywhere.'
  • 及郡，乃诣太守，说如此 - Means 'when he reached the county, he went to the governor and told him about it.'
  • 太守刘歆，即遣人随之往，寻向所志，不复得也 - Means 'Governor Liu Xun immediately sent people to follow him, searching for the marks he had made, but they could not find the way again.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 晋太元中 - Refers to the reign period of Emperor Hui of Jin, a time of relative peace and prosperity.
  • 秦难 - Refers to the Qin dynasty's harsh rule, often associated with the tyranny of Qin Shi Huang.

Proper Nouns

  • 晋太元中 - The reign period of Emperor Hui of Jin.
  • 武陵人 - The fisherman from Wuling.
  • 太守刘歆 - The governor Liu Xun.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 好游山水 - Classical expression meaning 'to enjoy traveling and exploring mountains and rivers.'
  • 深入忘反 - Literally 'delve deeply and forget to return,' meaning to get so engrossed in exploration that one loses track of time.
  • 石 - Refers to stone chambers or caves; the character  (likely a typographical error or variant of 窟) means 'cave' or 'hole.'
  • 水南有二石，一闭一开 - Describes a scene where two stone caves on the south side of the river are either closed or open.
  • 欲还，失道 - Means 'intending to return, lost the way,' indicating that the person got disoriented.
  • 或说中皆仙方、灵药及诸杂物 - A statement about someone claiming that the caves contain magical formulas, elixirs, and various objects.
  • 欲更寻索，不复知处矣 - Means 'wanting to search again, but no longer knowing the location,' indicating that the person cannot find the place anymore.
  • 尝采药至衡山 - Means 'once gathered herbs to Hengshan Mountain.'
  • 水深广，不得渡 - Describes a deep and wide river that cannot be crossed.
  • 仅得还家 - Means 'barely managed to return home.'
  • 伐薪人 - Refers to a woodcutter.
  • 问径 - Means 'asked for the way' or 'inquired about the path.'
  • 可试如水中看何由尔 - Means 'you can try going into the water to see how it is.'
  • 入穴 - Means 'enter the cave.'
  • 穴才容人 - Means 'the cave is just big enough for a person.'
  • 行数十步 - Means 'walked several dozen steps.'
  • 开明朗 - Means 'opened up into a bright and spacious area.'
  • 金石、丝竹之响 - Refers to the sounds of metal, stone, strings, and bamboo, symbolizing musical and harmonious sounds.
  • 目岩 - Refers to a rock formation that resembles eyes.
  • 相向如鸱尾 - Means 'facing each other like the tail of a kite' or 'facing each other like the tail of a mythical bird.'
  • 石室数十所 - Means 'dozens of stone chambers.'
  • 经过皆闻有金石、丝竹之响 - Means 'those who pass by all hear the sounds of metal, stone, strings, and bamboo.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南阳 - Refers to Nanyang, a region in Henan Province.
  • 衡山 - Refers to Mount Heng, one of the Five Sacred Mountains of China.
  • 长沙醴陵县 - Refers to Liling County in Changsha, Hunan Province.
  • 平乐县 - Refers to Pingle County, possibly in Guangxi or Guangdong.
  • 目岩 - A specific rock formation with a unique appearance.
  • 机山东有两岩 - Refers to two rock formations on Jishan Mountain.
  • 石室 - Refers to natural stone chambers or caves.
  • 金石、丝竹之响 - Symbolizes the sounds of nature and music, often associated with serene and harmonious environments.

Proper Nouns

  • 南阳 - Nanyang, a region in Henan Province.
  • 衡山 - Mount Heng, one of the Five Sacred Mountains of China.
  • 长沙醴陵县 - Liling County in Changsha, Hunan Province.
  • 平乐县 - Pingle County, possibly in Guangxi or Guangdong.
  • 目岩 - A specific rock formation with a unique appearance.
  • 机山东有两岩 - Two rock formations on Jishan Mountain.
  • 石室 - Natural stone chambers or caves.
  • 金石、丝竹之响 - The sounds of metal, stone, strings, and bamboo.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 中宿縣 - A specific county name in China, which should be kept as is in the translation.
  • 貞女 - Can be translated as 'chaste maiden' or 'virtuous girl.'
  • 秦世 - Refers to the Qin dynasty.
  • 螺 - Shellfish, specifically referring to abalone in this context.
  • 風雨昼昏 - A phrase describing a stormy day with heavy rain and darkness.
  • 化為此石 - Means 'transformed into this stone.'
  • 蓋山 - A specific mountain name, which should be kept as is in the translation.
  • 姑舒泉 - A specific spring name, which should be kept as is in the translation.
  • 舒女 - Refers to a girl named Shu.
  • 析薪 - Means 'to chop firewood.'
  • 牽挽不動 - Means 'could not be moved by pulling.'
  • 弦歌 - Refers to playing stringed instruments while singing.
  • 清泉湛然 - Means 'clear spring water, serene and still.'
  • 好音樂 - Means 'likes music.'
  • 朱鲤 - Red carp, often associated with auspiciousness in Chinese culture.
  • 嬉戏 - Means 'play' or 'recreation.'
  • 涌出 - Means 'erupts' or 'pours out.'
  • 吳舍人名猛 - Refers to a person named Wu Meng, who was a scholar-official.
  • 有道術 - Means 'possessed of magical arts.'
  • 同縣 - Means 'in the same county.'
  • 邹惠政 - A specific person's name, which should be kept as is in the translation.
  • 夜于家中庭烧香 - Means 'burned incense in the courtyard at night.'
  • 虎抱政儿超篱去 - Means 'a tiger carried away Zheng's child over the fence.'
  • 无所苦 - Means 'no harm done.'
  • 须臾当还 - Means 'will return shortly.'
  • 数十步 - Means 'several dozen steps.'
  • 号恸不去 - Means 'cried bitterly and did not leave.'
  • 蜀贼 - Refers to bandits from the Shu region.
  • 纵暴 - Means 'committed acts of violence.'
  • 发掘坟垅 - Means 'excavated graves.'
  • 民人进窜 - Means 'people fled.'
  • 号恸 - Means 'cried bitterly.'
  • 感怆 - Means 'moved to sorrow.'
  • 遂不犯 - Means 'therefore did not attack.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 貞女 - In Chinese culture, '貞女' refers to a woman who remains chaste and virtuous, often used in legends and stories.
  • 秦世 - The Qin dynasty (221-206 BCE) was known for its strict laws and centralized government.
  • 螺 - Abalone is a type of shellfish that was often consumed by ancient Chinese people.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 從武當山還 - Formal expression meaning 'returning from Mount Wudang,' indicating the location and context of the events.
  • 有言及左元放為曹公致鲈鱼者 - Phrase meaning 'there was mention of Zuo Yuanfang providing perch to Cao Cao,' where '致' means 'to provide' or 'to send.'
  • 此可得尔 - Colloquial expression meaning 'this is possible,' indicating a response to the previous statement.
  • 求大瓮盛水 - Phrase meaning 'asked for a large jar to hold water,' where '盛' means 'to hold' or 'to contain.'
  • 朱书符投水中 - Phrase meaning 'wrote a red talisman and threw it into the water,' where '朱书' means 'red writing' and '符' means 'talisman.'
  • 俄有一鲤鱼鼓鳍水中 - Phrase meaning 'soon there was a carp flapping its fins in the water,' where '俄' means 'soon' and '鼓鳍' means 'flapping fins.'
  • 有秘术 - Phrase meaning 'has secret techniques,' where '秘术' refers to esoteric or mystical abilities.
  • 尝就人借瓜刀 - Phrase meaning 'once borrowed a melon knife from someone,' where '尝' means 'once' and '借' means 'to borrow.'
  • 其主求子 - Phrase meaning 'the owner asked for the return of the knife,' where '求子' means 'asked for the return.'
  • 当即相还耳 - Phrase meaning 'I will return it immediately,' where '当即' means 'immediately' and '相还' means 'to return.'
  • 既而刀主行至嘉兴 - Phrase meaning 'afterward, the owner of the knife traveled to Jiaxing,' where '既而' means 'afterward' and '行至' means 'traveled to.'
  • 有鱼跃入船中 - Phrase meaning 'a fish jumped into the boat,' where '跃入' means 'jumped into.'
  • 破鱼腹，得瓜刀 - Phrase meaning 'opened the fish's belly and found the melon knife,' where '破' means 'to open' and '得' means 'to find.'
  • 太兴中 - Phrase meaning 'during the Tai Xing period,' referring to a specific era in Chinese history.
  • 衡阳区纯作鼠市 - Phrase meaning 'Hengyang Qu Chun made a mouse market,' where '作' means 'to make' and '鼠市' means 'mouse market.'
  • 四方丈余 - Phrase meaning 'four sides measuring over a zhang,' where '丈余' means 'over a zhang' (a unit of length).
  • 开四门 - Phrase meaning 'with four doors,' where '开' means 'to open' and '门' means 'doors.'
  • 门有一木人 - Phrase meaning 'each door had a wooden figure,' where '木人' means 'wooden figure.'
  • 纵四五鼠于中 - Phrase meaning 'released four or five mice inside,' where '纵' means 'to release' and '于中' means 'inside.'
  • 欲出门，木人辄以手推之 - Phrase meaning 'if they tried to leave, the wooden figure would push them back,' where '辄' means 'always' and '推之' means 'push them back.'
  • 晋大司马桓温，字元子 - Formal introduction meaning 'Duke Huan Wen of Jin, courtesy name Yuanzi,' where '大司马' means 'Grand Marshal' and '字' means 'courtesy name.'
  • 末年 - Phrase meaning 'in his later years,' where '末年' means 'in the final years.'
  • 忽有一比丘尼 - Phrase meaning 'suddenly there was a nun,' where '忽' means 'suddenly' and '比丘尼' means 'nun.'
  • 失其名 - Phrase meaning 'whose name was unknown,' where '失' means 'lost' and '其名' means 'her name.'
  • 来自远方 - Phrase meaning 'from a distant place,' where '来自' means 'from' and '远方' means 'distant place.'
  • 投温为檀越 - Phrase meaning 'offered herself to Huan Wen as a patron,' where '投' means 'to offer' and '檀越' means 'patron.'
  • 尼才行不恒 - Phrase meaning 'the nun's conduct was not constant,' where '才行' means 'conduct' and '不恒' means 'not constant.'
  • 愠其敬待 - Phrase meaning 'resented his respectful treatment,' where '愠' means 'resented' and '敬待' means 'respectful treatment.'
  • 居之门内 - Phrase meaning 'lived in his inner quarters,' where '居' means 'to live' and '门内' means 'inner quarters.'
  • 尼每浴，必至移时 - Phrase meaning 'the nun always bathed for a long time,' where '每' means 'always' and '移时' means 'for a long time.'
  • 温疑而窥之 - Phrase meaning 'Huan Wen suspected and peeked,' where '疑' means 'suspected' and '窥' means 'to peek.'
  • 见尼裸身挥刀，破腹出脏，断截身首，支分脔切 - Phrase meaning 'saw the nun naked, swinging a knife, opening her belly, removing her organs, cutting off her head and body, slicing them into pieces,' where '裸身' means 'naked,' '挥刀' means 'swinging a knife,' '破腹' means 'opening her belly,' '出脏' means 'removing her organs,' '断截' means 'cutting off,' '身首' means 'head and body,' and '支分脔切' means 'slicing into pieces.'
  • 温怪骇而还 - Phrase meaning 'Huan Wen was startled and returned,' where '怪骇' means 'startled' and '还' means 'returned.'
  • 及至尼出浴室，身形如常 - Phrase meaning 'when the nun emerged from the bath, her body was as usual,' where '及至' means 'when' and '身形如常' means 'body as usual.'
  • 温以实问 - Phrase meaning 'Huan Wen asked her about the truth,' where '以实问' means 'asked about the truth.'
  • 尼答曰 - Phrase meaning 'the nun replied,' where '答曰' means 'replied.'
  • 若逐凌君上，形当如之 - Phrase meaning 'if I were to harm your lord, my body would be like this,' where '逐凌' means 'to harm' and '形当如之' means 'my body would be like this.'
  • 时温方谋问鼎 - Phrase meaning 'at that time, Huan Wen was plotting to seize power,' where '方谋' means 'plotting' and '问鼎' means 'to seize power.'
  • 闻之怅然 - Phrase meaning 'he heard this and was greatly disappointed,' where '闻之' means 'heard this' and '怅然' means 'greatly disappointed.'
  • 故以戒惧，终守臣节 - Phrase meaning 'therefore, he was warned and ultimately maintained his loyalty as a minister,' where '故以' means 'therefore' and '终守臣节' means 'ultimately maintained his loyalty as a minister.'
  • 尼后辞去，不知所在 - Phrase meaning 'the nun later left and her whereabouts are unknown,' where '辞去' means 'left' and '不知所在' means 'her whereabouts are unknown.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 武当山 - Refers to Mount Wudang, a famous Taoist mountain in China.
  • 左元放 - A historical figure known for his mystical abilities.
  • 曹公 - Refers to Cao Cao, a prominent warlord and statesman during the late Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 鲈鱼 - A type of fish, often associated with literary and cultural significance.
  • 秘术 - Refers to esoteric or mystical techniques.
  • 瓜刀 - A knife used for cutting melons.
  • 嘉兴 - A city in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 太兴中 - Refers to the Tai Xing period, a specific era in Chinese history.
  • 衡阳 - A city in Hunan Province, China.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '后以鸡鸣出去，平早潜归，于篱外窃窥其家中' describes a series of actions: leaving early, returning secretly, and peeping into the house. The actions are linked by the temporal markers '后' (after) and '平早' (early in the morning).
  • The sequence '见一少女，从瓮中出，至灶下燃火' describes the girl's actions: coming out of the jar, going to the stove, and lighting a fire. The actions are linked by the verb '至' (to).
  • The sequence '端便入门，径至瓮所视螺，但见女' describes Duan's actions: entering the house, going directly to the jar, and seeing the girl. The actions are linked by the verb '径至' (directly went to).
  • The clause '乃到灶下问之曰' introduces Duan's question to the girl, which follows his observation of her.
  • The clause '女大惶惑，欲还瓮中，不能得去' describes the girl's emotional state and her attempt to return to the jar.
  • The clause '答曰，我天汉中白水素女也' introduces the girl's identity as the White Water Maiden.
  • The clause '天帝哀卿少孤，恭慎自守，故使我权为守舍炊烹' explains the reason for the girl's presence and her role in Duan's life.
  • The clause '十年之中，使卿居富得妇，自当还去' outlines the future plan for Duan and the girl's eventual departure.
  • The clause '而卿无故窃相窥掩，吾形已见，不宜复留，当相委去' explains the girl's decision to leave due to Duan's actions.
  • The clause '虽然，尔后自当少差，勤于田作，渔采治生' offers a consolation and advice to Duan.
  • The clause '留此壳去，以贮米谷，常不可乏' describes the gift the girl leaves behind.
  • The clause '端请留，终不肯' describes Duan's attempt to persuade the girl to stay.
  • The clause '时天忽风雨，翕然而去' describes the girl's sudden departure.
  • The clause '端为立神座，时节祭祀' describes Duan's response to the girl's departure.
  • The clause '居常饶足，不致大富耳' describes Duan's subsequent life.
  • The clause '于是乡人以女妻之' describes the villagers' intervention in Duan's life.
  • The clause '后任至令长，云今道中素女祠是也' describes Duan's career advancement and the location of the White Water Maiden temple.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 宗渊字叔林 - Standard biographical formula meaning 'Zong Yuan, courtesy name Shulin.'
  • 晋太元中 - Refers to the period during the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin (376–396 CE).
  • 为寻阳太守 - Means 'served as the governor of Xunyang (Jiujiang).'
  • 付厨 - Means 'gave to the kitchen' or 'assigned to the kitchen.'
  • 旦且以二头作□，便著潘汁，□中养之 - Elliptical phrase requiring clarification: '□' likely refers to a dish or container. '作□' means 'to prepare' or 'to cook.' '著潘汁' means 'to pour on millet juice.' '□中养之' means 'to raise them in the dish.'
  • 其暮梦有十丈夫，并著乌布裤褶 - '其暮' means 'that night.' '十丈夫' means 'ten men.' '并著乌布裤褶' means 'all wearing black cloth trousers and jackets.'
  • 自反缚，向宗渊叩首，若求哀 - '自反缚' means 'bound themselves.' '向宗渊叩首' means 'bowing to Zong Yuan.' '若求哀' means 'as if pleading for mercy.'
  • 明旦，厨人宰二龟 - '明旦' means 'the next morning.' '厨人' means 'the cook.' '宰二龟' means 'slaughtered two turtles.'
  • 其暮复梦八人求哀如初 - '其暮' means 'that night.' '复梦' means 'had another dream.' '八人求哀如初' means 'eight men pleading for mercy as before.'
  • 宗渊方悟，令勿杀 - '宗渊方悟' means 'Zong Yuan finally realized.' '令勿杀' means 'ordered not to kill.'
  • 明夜还梦见八人来，跪谢恩 - '明夜还梦见' means 'the next night, he had another dream.' '八人来' means 'eight men came.' '跪谢恩' means 'kneeling to thank him.'
  • 遂不复食龟 - Means 'thereafter, he no longer ate turtle.'
  • 熊无穴，居大树孔中 - '熊无穴' means 'bears have no dens.' '居大树孔中' means 'live in the holes of big trees.'
  • 东土呼熊为子路 - '东土' means 'eastern lands.' '呼熊为子路' means 'call bears 'Zilu.'
  • 于是便下 - '于是' means 'then.' '便下' means 'they would descend.'
  • 不呼则不动也 - Means 'if not called, they do not move.'
  • 鄱阳县民黄赭，入山采荆杨子，遂迷不知道 - '鄱阳县民' means 'a resident of Poyang County.' '黄赭' is a person's name. '入山采荆杨子' means 'went into the mountains to collect *Platycladus orientalis* seeds.' '遂迷不知道' means 'became lost and did not know the way.'
  • 数曰，饥饿，忽见一大龟 - '数曰' means 'after several days.' '饥饿' means 'hungry.' '忽见一大龟' means 'suddenly saw a large turtle.'
  • 龟即回右转，赭即从行 - '龟即回右转' means 'the turtle immediately turned right.' '赭即从行' means 'Zhang Zhe followed.'
  • 去十馀里，便至溪水，见贾客行船 - '去十馀里' means 'after traveling more than ten miles.' '便至溪水' means 'they arrived at a stream.' '见贾客行船' means 'saw merchants sailing.'
  • 赭即往乞食，便语船人曰 - '赭即往乞食' means 'Zhang Zhe went to beg for food.' '便语船人曰' means 'then spoke to the boatmen.'
  • 我向者於溪边见一龟，甚大，可共往取之 - '我向者於溪边见一龟' means 'I saw a turtle by the stream earlier.' '甚大' means 'very large.' '可共往取之' means 'we can go together to catch it.'
  • 言讫，面即生疮 - '言讫' means 'after he finished speaking.' '面即生疮' means 'his face immediately broke out in sores.'
  • 既往，亦复不见龟 - '既往' means 'after going.' '亦复不见龟' means 'they also did not see the turtle.'
  • 还家数曰，病疮而死 - '还家数曰' means 'after returning home for several days.' '病疮而死' means 'died from the sores.'
